formation_en: Move the known object after 把 and put its resulting place after the verb.
usage_en: Focuses on where the action causes the object to be or arrive.

pattern: Subject + 把 + Object 1 + Transfer verb + (给) + Object 2
formation_en: Put the transferred item after 把 and the recipient after the transfer verb or 给.
usage_en: Highlights what is transferred while retaining the recipient after the verb.

pattern: Subject + 把 + Object + Verb + Result/Directional/State complement
formation_en: Place the known object after 把 and complete the verb with the appropriate complement.
usage_en: Makes the object's affected outcome central to the sentence.

pattern: Patient + 被 + Agent + Verb + Other element
formation_en: Place the affected patient first and the responsible agent after 被.
usage_en: Presents the event from the patient's perspective while retaining the agent.

formation_en: Place 被 directly before the completed predicate when no agent is named.
usage_en: Keeps attention on the affected patient and outcome.

formation_en: Add 着 to the accompanying first verb and place the main action after it.
usage_en: Shows the first posture or action continuing while the second action occurs.

pattern: A + 比 + B + 更/还 + Adjective
formation_en: Place 更 or 还 between the comparison standard B and the adjective.
usage_en: Adds intensified degree to a basic 比 comparison.

formation_en: Place the comparison standard B between 跟 and 一样.
usage_en: States that A and B match in the relevant understood respect.

pattern: A + 跟 + B + 一样 + Adjective
formation_en: Put the shared quality directly after 一样.
usage_en: States equality specifically along the dimension named by the adjective.

formation_en: Place 不 before 比 and put the comparison standard B before the adjective.
usage_en: Rejects a claim that A exceeds B on the named quality.

pattern: A + 比 + B + 多/少/早/晚 + Verb + Quantity
formation_en: Place the direction of difference before the verb and its measurement after the verb.
usage_en: Quantifies a difference in action amount or event timing.

pattern: Place + Verb + Direction/Result + (了) + Number-Measure + New entity
formation_en: Set the location first, then report the appearance and introduce the new entity last.
usage_en: Presents an entity as newly entering or becoming present in the scene.

pattern: Place + Verb + Result + 了 + Number-Measure + Entity
formation_en: Put the source place first, use a completed disappearance predicate, and place the affected entity last.
usage_en: Updates an established scene by reporting what is no longer present there.

formation_en: Place the emphasized judgment or stance between 是 and 的.
usage_en: Strengthens the speaker's commitment to that evaluation or position.

formation_en: State the verb-object phrase, repeat the same verb, and attach the complement to the copy.
usage_en: Allows both a direct object and a degree, state, or quantity complement in one predicate.

pattern: 先 + Action 1, 再/然后 + Action 2
formation_en: Place 先 before the first action and 再 or 然后 before the following action.
usage_en: Makes the temporal order between two actions explicit.

pattern: 或者 + Alternative 1, 或者 + Alternative 2
formation_en: Place 或者 before each parallel alternative.
usage_en: Shows that either alternative is possible or available.

pattern: 一会儿 + State/Action 1, 一会儿 + State/Action 2
formation_en: Place 一会儿 before each alternating state or action.
usage_en: Presents the subject as switching back and forth between the two.

pattern: Subject + 又 + Predicate 1 + 又 + Predicate 2
formation_en: Place 又 before each parallel predicate.
usage_en: Affirms that both predicates hold for the same subject.

pattern: Subject + 一边 + Action 1 + 一边 + Action 2
formation_en: Place 一边 before each of the two concurrent actions.
usage_en: Presents both actions as unfolding during the same period.

pattern: 不但 + Point 1, 而且 + Point 2
formation_en: Place 不但 before the first point and 而且 before the added point.
usage_en: Builds emphasis by extending the first statement with additional information.

pattern: 虽然 + Conceded fact, 可是 + Contrasting result
formation_en: Place 虽然 before the acknowledged fact and 可是 before the main contrasting result.
usage_en: Shows that the second fact holds in spite of the first.

pattern: 如果 + Condition, Subject + 就 + Result
formation_en: Put 如果 before the hypothetical condition and 就 before the consequence.
usage_en: Links a possible situation to what will happen or be done in that case.

pattern: Condition + 的话, Subject + 就 + Result
formation_en: Put 的话 at the end of the condition and 就 before the result predicate.
usage_en: Creates an explicit hypothetical link while marking the condition at its end.

pattern: 只有 + Condition, Subject + 才 + Result
formation_en: Place 只有 before the required condition and 才 before the result.
usage_en: Restricts the result to cases in which the condition is satisfied.

pattern: 只要 + Condition, Subject + 就 + Result
formation_en: Place 只要 before the sufficient condition and 就 before the result.
usage_en: Presents the condition as enough to guarantee or license the consequence.

formation_en: Place 为了 before the intended goal and put the means or action in the following clause.
usage_en: Explains what desired outcome motivates the action.

pattern: Verb + 了 + Object, Subject + 就 + Next action
formation_en: Mark the first event complete with 了 and place 就 before the following action.
usage_en: Compresses a condition or temporal sequence and emphasizes a direct transition.

formation_en: Mark the trigger event with 了 and state the following action directly.
usage_en: Uses the completed first event as the understood condition or time for the next action.

formation_en: Place 大概 directly before the estimated quantity.
usage_en: Signals that the number is a reasonable estimate, not an exact count.

pattern: Adjacent numeral + Adjacent numeral + Measure + Noun
formation_en: Place two consecutive numerals directly together before one shared measure word.
usage_en: Expresses an approximate quantity spanning the two nearby values.
